data.title: Microservices Architecture
data.description: YouTube's backend is composed of numerous microservices that handle specific functionalities such as video playback, recommendation engine, and user data management.
data.technical_spec: Distributed service-oriented architecture built using containers and orchestrated by Kubernetes.

data.title: Content Delivery Network (CDN)
data.description: To ensure quick video loading times globally, YouTube uses CDNs extensively to store and deliver video content close to users.
data.technical_spec: Akamai, Google Edge Network; distributed server systems globally.

data.title: Load Balancing
data.description: Uses load balancing to distribute network or application traffic across many servers to improve the reliability of applications.
data.technical_spec: Global load distribution algorithms including round-robin and least connections.

data.title: Scalable Databases
data.description: Manages vast amounts of data generated by users, videos, interactions, and logs using scalable database solutions.
data.technical_spec: Combination of SQL databases (Spanner) and NoSQL solutions (Bigtable).

data.title: RESTful APIs
data.description: APIs designed to be stateless and allow external services to interface with core YouTube functionalities.
data.technical_spec: Implemented using HTTPS standard protocols; supports API key and OAuth 2.0 for authentication.
